# SolDecode

> Chrome extension that intercepts Solana wallet sign requests, simulates the transaction on-chain, and shows you exactly what will happen to your wallet before you sign. Drainers, rug pulls, infinite-approval traps, and address-poisoning attempts get caught in the preview, not in your portfolio.

SolDecode is a defensive layer for Solana users. It sits between your wallet and the dapp at sign time, parses the pending transaction, simulates it on a recent slot, and renders a plain-English summary of the net effect on your wallet: which tokens move, where they go, and whether anything looks like a known phishing pattern.

## Key pages
- [Home](https://jvr0x.github.io/soldecode-extension/): product overview, screenshots, install link

## What it catches
- Infinite-approval drainers (any Approve/ApproveChecked with amount = u64::MAX)
- Spoofed token mints (Unicode look-alikes, fake USDC/USDT/JUP, etc.) matched against a canonical mint table
- Address-poisoning attempts via similar-looking destination addresses
- Net-negative token flows that the user did not intend
- Failed-tx reasons rendered in plain English instead of cryptic error codes

## How it works
- Intercepts the signTransaction / signAllTransactions request from the connected wallet
- Simulates the transaction against a recent Solana slot via RPC
- Parses the resulting balance changes and instructions
- Surfaces a human-readable preview before the wallet's own sign prompt
- Lets the user reject if the preview does not match the intent of the action they triggered on the dapp

## Tech
- Chrome extension (Manifest V3)
- Static marketing site at https://jvr0x.github.io/soldecode-extension/
- Solana RPC via Helius for parsed transaction data

## Install
- Chrome Web Store: see install link on the home page

## Contact
- Author: @jvr0x on X
- Issues: https://github.com/jvr0x/soldecode-extension/issues
- Privacy policy: https://jvr0x.github.io/soldecode-extension/PRIVACY.md
